description: Written to the judge Hananel b. Shemuel, this letter mentions the son of the Nagid (Avraham Maimonides, his son-in-law) and a certain R. Menahem. The judge, accompanied by the young son of the Nagid, had visited a village and summoned complainants to Fustat. The writer (probably a stranger) discusses the maneuvers of 'those people' who had formed a mob and threatened to beat the complainants if they should come to Fustat. (Information from Goitein index cards)
